compiled_state.title: As a developer, sprint executes recipe: tasks as deterministic shell commands
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Sprint recognizes recipe: task type, invokes recipe-executor.sh, captures structured JSON output, and records results in the sprint log.
**Why**: Recipe tasks bypass sub-agent dispatch — they execute deterministically via shell commands, saving tokens and eliminating variance.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint task dispatch recognizes recipe: type, invokes recipe-executor.sh with recipe name + parameters, captures structured JSON (files_changed, transforms_applied, errors, exit_code), records results in sprint log, git stash snapshot before execution for rollback
- OUT: Cleanup phase (S3), pre-flight (S4), fallback (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint log shows deterministic command execution for recipe tasks with structured JSON results
  ← Satisfies: "Sprint executes recipe: tasks as shell commands with the recipe executor"
- When this story is complete, recipe task results include a human-readable summary translatable to the completion-verifier's expected input format (not raw executor JSON)
  ← Satisfies: completion-verifier compatibility (adversarial review finding)
- When this story is complete, executor non-zero exit / partial JSON / timeout triggers git stash rollback and records a structured error in the sprint log — the sprint does not crash or hang
  ← Satisfies: mid-execution failure handling (adversarial review finding)
- When this story is complete, recipe execution uses git stash snapshot so the orchestrator can revert to pre-recipe state on review rejection
  ← Satisfies: rollback capability for wrong recipe output (adversarial review finding)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] /dso:sprint is a safeguard file — edits require explicit user approval per Never-Do rule 18
- [Reliability] Git stash snapshot before recipe execution enables rollback for both mid-execution failures and post-review rejection
- [Testing] Completion-verifier sub-agent is designed for LLM output — recipe results need a translation layer to produce verifier-compatible summaries

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
